feat(security): evaluate tree-sitter for AST-based vulnerability detection
related_issues.relevance: Target issue - evaluate tree-sitter for AST-based detection

constraints: Hybrid approach recommended in issue - keep regex for simple patterns, add tree-sitter for data-flow
14 patterns need conversion from regex to tree-sitter queries
Binary size increase from grammar crates (~5-10 languages)
test_coverage: Unit tests in patterns.rs (10+ tests covering pattern loading, API key detection, SQL injection, path traversal, weak crypto, XSS, extension filtering). Integration tests in security_integration.rs (4 tests including multi-line limitation test documenting known gap).

library_findings.relevant_api: Parser, Language, Tree, Query - parse() returns Tree, query() for pattern matching
library_findings.notes: Current version in issue scope; verify via Context7 for 0.24 API surface

library_findings.relevant_api: rust_language() returns Language, used with Parser::set_language()
library_findings.notes: Language-specific grammar crate; need ~5-10 languages (rust, python, javascript, typescript, java, php)

library_findings.relevant_api: Regex::new(), find() - currently used in PatternEngine
library_findings.notes: Existing dependency; regex will remain for simple patterns in hybrid approach

approaches.name: Hybrid Scanner (Regex + Tree-sitter)
approaches.description: Add tree-sitter as secondary scanner alongside existing regex engine. Regex handles single-line patterns (API keys, weak crypto). Tree-sitter handles multi-line data-flow patterns (SQL injection source->sink). New AstPatternEngine module with language-specific parsers.
pros: Incremental adoption - regex scanner stays operational
Tree-sitter incremental parsing is fast on edits
Existing patterns continue working without rewrite
cons: Learning curve for tree-sitter query syntax
Data-flow tracking still requires custom logic on top of AST

approaches.name: Full Migration to Tree-sitter
approaches.description: Replace regex PatternEngine entirely with tree-sitter-based AstPatternEngine. Convert all 14 patterns to tree-sitter queries. Remove regex dependency.

approaches.name: Minimal Spike - Tree-sitter for SQL Injection Only
approaches.description: Add tree-sitter-rust as spike to detect multi-line SQL injection. Keep regex scanner unchanged. If spike successful, expand to other multi-line patterns.
pros: Easy to abandon if issues arise
cons: Limited scope may not justify grammar crate size

recommendation: Hybrid Scanner approach - adds tree-sitter for multi-line detection while preserving existing regex scanner. This matches the issue's suggested approach and minimizes risk. Start with tree-sitter-rust for SQL injection (addresses test_multi_line_vulnerability_not_detected gap), then expand to other data-flow patterns. Files touched: security/mod.rs (add AstScanner), new security/ast.rs (~300 lines), update Cargo.toml (add tree-sitter, tree-sitter-rust), update scanner.rs (orchestrate both).
